landing.heroSub2: for developers and enterprise teams. Focus on your product, not on integrations.
landing.featuresSub: Solving real API management problems developers face in production
landing.feat1Desc: OpenAI-compatible unified interface. Our platform handles multi-channel routing and billing analytics automatically, eliminating the overhead of managing individual provider accounts, rate limits, and separate billing.
landing.feat2Desc: When an upstream channel experiences failure or abnormal latency, the platform automatically switches to backup channels within 0.1 seconds, ensuring your business never stops due to single-point failures.
landing.feat3Desc: Bind each API Key to a project name + monthly budget. CSV export includes cost, request path, IP, and post-deduction balance — perfect for accounting, cost allocation, and budget alerts.
landing.feat4Desc: Real-time balance sync + full database audit trail. Every transaction is verifiable, and funds are safeguarded.
landing.pricingSub: Pay only for what you use, no hidden fees
landing.compareSub: Why B2B cannot rely on cheap gray-market channels
landing.faqA1: Sign up, top up via redeem code, then create an API key in the dashboard. Whole process under 5 minutes — no overseas credit card needed.
landing.faqA2Pre: We provide a unified API gateway with platform-level management capabilities, including multi-channel routing, billing analytics, key management, rate limiting, and quotas. Compared to direct upstream integration, TransitAI is
landing.faqA2Post: , ideal for growing teams and SaaS projects.
landing.faqA3: Buy a redeem code on Xianyu (Chinese marketplace), then enter it on the Recharge page. Instant credit. Tiers: $100 / $300 / $500 / $1000 / $3000.
landing.faqQ4: What platform capabilities do you offer?
landing.faqA4: TransitAI provides a unified API gateway, multi-channel routing, request analytics, billing management, CSV export, Playground for testing, key permission controls, and rate limiting/quotas. Platform credits give you access to all features.
landing.faqA5Pre: Fully compatible with OpenAI SDK format (i.e.
landing.faqA5Post: endpoint). Existing code only needs base_url and api_key changes.
landing.faqA6: Regular top-up is pay-as-you-go for light use. Membership unlocks higher RPM/TPM limits and more API keys — for teams with batch requests.
landing.ctaBottomSub: Sign up to receive trial credit. No credit card required.
landing.footerDisclaimer: TransitAI is an independent API gateway service and is not affiliated with, endorsed by, or sponsored by OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, DeepSeek, Alibaba, or any of the AI model providers mentioned. All model names and trademarks belong to their respective owners.

apiDocs.title: API Docs
apiDocs.quickstep1: Step 1: Sign up and get your API Key
apiDocs.quickstep1desc: Register → Top up balance → Go to 「API Keys」 page to create a key and copy the sk- secret.
apiDocs.quickstep2desc: We support both OpenAI-compatible format (recommended) and native Anthropic format. Pick one.
apiDocs.quickstep3desc: Replace YOUR_API_KEY in the examples below with your real key and run it.
apiDocs.overviewDesc: TransitAI is compatible with both OpenAI SDK format and native Anthropic format. No business logic changes needed — just update base_url and api_key.
apiDocs.authDesc: All requests must include your API Key in the request header:
apiDocs.authDescAnthropic: Native Anthropic format uses the x-api-key header:
apiDocs.authTip: Create and manage keys on the 「API Keys」 page
apiDocs.openaiFormatDesc: Fully compatible with the OpenAI API. Just update base_url and api_key — no other code changes needed.
apiDocs.streamDesc: Set stream: true to enable SSE streaming output for real-time display.
apiDocs.anthropicFormatDesc: Use the official Anthropic SDK directly. Just update base_url. Supports all native Claude parameters (system prompts, tool use, vision, etc.).
apiDocs.anthropicNote: Note: The base_url for native Anthropic format does NOT include /v1 — the SDK appends it automatically.
apiDocs.modelListDesc: Call the following endpoint to get all available models and their pricing:
apiDocs.errorsDesc: When a request fails, the API returns a standard JSON error body:
apiDocs.billLi1: All prices are in USD, charged per 1,000,000 (1M) tokens
apiDocs.billLi2: Input and output tokens are billed separately — see 「Models」 page for pricing
apiDocs.billLi3: Requests return 402 when balance is insufficient — please top up
apiDocs.billLi4: Monthly budget alerts available in API Key settings
apiDocs.billLi5: Every charge is logged — view details on the 「Billing」 page
apiDocs.mcpDesc: TransitAI is fully compatible with MCP (Model Context Protocol). Configure it as your Claude Desktop API source:
apiDocs.tutorialsDesc: Pick your favorite tool and configure it to use the TransitAI Claude API.
apiDocs.tutCCdesc: Anthropic's official CLI tool. Switch to TransitAI via environment variables.
apiDocs.tutCCStep2: 2. Set environment variables (add to ~/.bashrc or ~/.zshrc to persist)
apiDocs.tutCCStep4: Done! The cc command now uses your TransitAI balance with identical UX.
apiDocs.tutSwitchDesc: Switch between multiple Claude Code API providers without manually editing env vars.
apiDocs.tutSwitchHint: Then run ccswitch use transitai to switch to TransitAI, ccswitch use official to switch back.
apiDocs.tutClineDesc: One of the most powerful AI coding extensions in VS Code — supports MCP, file ops, and terminal commands.
apiDocs.tutClineStep1: 1. Install Cline (formerly Claude Dev) from the VS Code Extensions marketplace
apiDocs.tutClineStep2: 2. Click the Cline icon to open the sidebar, then click the ⚙️ settings icon
apiDocs.tutClineCfg5: Model: claude-mix (or any other available model)
apiDocs.tutClineStep4: 4. Save the settings — you can now use Cline normally
apiDocs.tutCursorDesc: Cursor uses Anthropic's official API by default. Configure it to use TransitAI:
apiDocs.tutCursorStep2: 2. Go to the Models tab, find the Anthropic API Key section
apiDocs.tutCursorStep4: 4. Click Verify — a green ✓ means success
apiDocs.tutCursorNote: Note: Some Cursor features (e.g. Agent) may still call the official API. Consider disabling telemetry under Settings → Privacy mode.
apiDocs.tutCommonNote: Reminder: Replace YOUR_API_KEY in all examples with your real key (starting with sk-) from the API Keys page.
apiDocs.ccFull1Title: Step 1: Install Node.js (skip if already installed)
apiDocs.ccFull1Desc: Claude Code requires Node.js 18+. Download the installer for your OS from the link below.
apiDocs.ccFull2Desc: Open terminal (Windows: PowerShell; Mac: Terminal; Linux: bash) and run:
apiDocs.ccFull3Title: Step 3: Configure TransitAI as API source
apiDocs.ccFull3Desc: Pick the env-var method matching your OS:
apiDocs.ccFull3Mac: Mac / Linux (recommend adding to ~/.zshrc to persist)
apiDocs.ccFull4Title: Step 4: Start in your project directory
apiDocs.ccFull4Desc: cd into your code project root and run cc to enter interactive mode:
apiDocs.ccFull5Desc: Claude Code shows an interactive terminal — just type what you want. Common commands:
apiDocs.ccFull5Tip: Claude Code automatically reads your project context and understands the codebase structure.
apiDocs.ccFull6Desc: After a few simple test queries (like 'Introduce Python'), check the TransitAI 「Billing」 page — you should see usage records.
apiDocs.swFull1Title: Step 1: Make sure Claude Code is installed
apiDocs.swFull1Desc: CCSwitch is an endpoint switcher for Claude Code. Install Claude Code first using the guide above.
apiDocs.swFull3Desc: Register TransitAI under the name transitai:
apiDocs.swFull4Desc: If you want to keep Anthropic official as a fallback:
apiDocs.swFull6Cmd2: ccswitch current — show the active endpoint
apiDocs.swFull6Cmd3: ccswitch remove transitai — remove an endpoint
apiDocs.clFull1Title: Step 1: Install VS Code (skip if already installed)
apiDocs.clFull1Desc: If VS Code is not installed, download it here:
apiDocs.clFull2Desc: Open VS Code → Extensions (Ctrl+Shift+X) → search Cline → click Install.
apiDocs.clFull2Note: 💡 Cline was previously called Claude Dev. They conflict — install only one.
apiDocs.clFull3Desc: Click the Cline icon (robot avatar) in the VS Code activity bar to open the sidebar.
apiDocs.clFull4Desc: Click the ⚙️ gear icon at the top right of the sidebar.
apiDocs.clFull5Cfg2: Anthropic API Key: paste your sk-xxx
apiDocs.clFull6Desc: Click Done to save. Back in the chat panel, describe what you want (e.g. 'refactor this file') and Cline will read your project and get to work.
apiDocs.clFull6Note: Cline auto-edits files and runs terminal commands. It asks for approval before each action — safe and controllable.
apiDocs.cuFull1Desc: Cursor is an AI-first code editor (forked from VS Code). Download here:
apiDocs.cuFull2Desc: After launching Cursor, press Cmd+, (Mac) or Ctrl+, (Windows) to open Settings.
apiDocs.cuFull3Title: Step 3: Go to the Models tab
apiDocs.cuFull3Desc: Find Models on the left (or via Cursor Settings → Models). Scroll down to the OpenAI API Key section.
apiDocs.cuFull5Desc: Click Verify — a green ✓ means it works.
apiDocs.cuFull6Desc: Press Cmd+K (Mac) / Ctrl+K (Windows) for inline AI, or Cmd+L for the chat panel.
apiDocs.cuFull6Note: ⚠️ Some advanced Cursor features (e.g. Composer Agent) may still call the official API. Consider disabling telemetry under Settings → Privacy mode for full routing.
apiDocs.compareDesc: TransitAI supports both OpenAI-compatible format and native Anthropic format. They run independently with the same pricing, but differ significantly in features.
apiDocs.compareCompatOpenAI: Very wide (NextChat, Dify, OpenWebUI, Cherry Studio, Cursor, etc.)
apiDocs.compareCompatAnthropic: Anthropic SDK / Claude Code / Cline / any native client
apiDocs.compareUseOpenAIDesc: You use NextChat / Dify / OpenWebUI / Cherry Studio / Cursor, or don't need cache / tool use / vision. OpenAI format is the simplest and most compatible.
apiDocs.compareUseAnthropicDesc: If you use Claude Code / Cline / Anthropic official SDK, or prefer the Anthropic native protocol, choose this format. Feature support depends on the aggregated upstream.
apiDocs.compareTip: 💡 Tip: The same API Key works for both formats simultaneously.
apiDocs.groupsDesc: Choose a group based on your needs. The model name suffix determines which group routes the request. Two sets with independent pricing and routing.
apiDocs.groupsExample: Example: switch group via suffix on the same model:
apiDocs.groupCodeDesc: Same code, just append -pro to the model name to switch to Official group. Two API formats × Two groups = 4 combinations
apiDocs.groupSwitchTip: Append -pro to the model name in any example to switch to Official direct group (e.g. claude-mix → claude-mix-pro)
apiDocs.guideDesc: Different models have different strengths and pricing. Pick the right model for your use case to balance quality and cost.
apiDocs.guideNoteCoding: Claude primary; auto-fallback to MiniMax 230B when Claude is unstable. Balanced capability, friendly price — great default choice.
apiDocs.guideNoteFast: Fast response, optimized for brief requests, autocomplete suggestions, command translation, etc.
apiDocs.guideNoteReason: Complex reasoning & long docs; Fable 5 supports 200K context
apiDocs.guideNoteEcon: Ultra-cheap input pricing, ideal for high-volume token tasks like embedding preprocessing or data labeling.
apiDocs.guideNoteChinese: Trained on extensive Chinese corpora — better handling of complex Chinese semantics and cultural context.
apiDocs.guideTip1: 💡 New to the platform? Start with claude-mix — balanced and compatible with most tools.
apiDocs.guideTip2: 💡 See "Models & Pricing" in the top menu for detailed input/output rates.
apiDocs.guideTip3: 💡 Need streaming output? See the 'Request Example' section below for stream: true usage.
apiDocs.guideExamplesDesc: After choosing a model, replace the model field in the example with any model name from the table above.
apiDocs.guideExampleHint: 💡 Replace "claude-mix" in the code with any model from the table above.
apiDocs.guideNoteVision: Recognize image content, ideal for screenshot analysis, OCR, form parsing. Gemini 2.5 Pro offers best value
apiDocs.guideNoteGeminiFlash: Balanced Gemini, speed + quality, multimodal, suits most tasks
apiDocs.guideNoteGeminiLite: Cheapest Gemini, ideal for mass requests, batch analysis
apiDocs.guideNoteGeminiPro: Complex reasoning, long-doc analysis, multimodal fusion, Google top-tier model
apiDocs.guideNoteGPT55: New GPT flagship, strong for coding & reasoning
apiDocs.tutPSDesc: Call gpt-image-2 directly inside Photoshop for image generation and editing. Free plugin, one-click install, auto-billed via your TransitAI balance.
apiDocs.psFull1Desc: Click the button below to download the installer (~824KB). Save to desktop.
apiDocs.psFull2Desc: Double-click .ccx → Creative Cloud install dialog appears → click "Install". Takes ~5 seconds. If dialog does not appear, ensure Adobe Creative Cloud desktop app is signed in.
apiDocs.psFull3Desc: Open Photoshop → top menu "Plugins" → "TransitAI Image" → click to launch. Plugin panel appears on the right.
apiDocs.psFull4Desc: Paste your TransitAI sk-key (created in "API Keys" page) into the API Key field. Click save.
apiDocs.psFull5Desc: Generate: type prompt → click "Generate". Edit: select a region in PS → type edit prompt → click "Edit Selection". Each call costs $0.665, auto-deducted from balance.
apiDocs.psFull6Note: ⚠️ Plugin forces transitai.cloud routing, OpenAI keys not accepted; ⚠️ Insufficient balance returns 402; ⚠️ Photoshop 24.0+ required (2023 or later).
apiDocs.guideNoteCodeReview: Optimized for code review, auto-detects bugs, security issues, style problems. Ideal for PR review, CI integration, code quality gating
apiDocs.guideNoteImage1K: 1K resolution, ¥0.15/img, cheapest. For batch generation, social media, idea drafts. Fast, good enough.
apiDocs.guideNoteImagePro: 2K resolution, ¥0.225/img, best value. For commercial illustration, product shots, posters.
apiDocs.guideNoteImage4K: 4K resolution, ¥0.30/img, highest quality. For large print, ad keyvisual, brand assets.
apiDocs.guideNoteEconAgg: Cheapest tier on Aitechflux, 400K context, ideal for batch
apiDocs.guideNoteMid: Mid-tier price, covers most chat / coding / long-doc tasks
apiDocs.guideNoteCodingAdv: Strong code planning & tool calls, for Cursor / Claude Code agents
apiDocs.guideNoteFable: Latest Anthropic flagship, complex reasoning & creative writing

changePwd.heroSub: For account security, change your password regularly

legal.termsBody:
TransitAI Terms of Service

Last updated: April 2026

Welcome to TransitAI (the "Platform"). Please read and fully understand these Terms before using the Platform. By registering or using any service, you acknowledge that you have read, understood, and agreed to be bound by these Terms in their entirety.

1. Service Description

The Platform provides API forwarding services for large language models including Anthropic Claude. The API is compatible with the OpenAI SDK, so you can call supported models using standard clients.

2. Account Registration and Use

1. You must register with a real, valid email address and are responsible for keeping your password secure.

2. You may not transfer or lend your account or API Keys to third parties. You are solely responsible for any losses resulting from unauthorized use of your account.

3. The Platform reserves the right to restrict, suspend, or terminate accounts that violate these Terms.

3. Billing and Price Transparency

1. All models are billed by actual token usage, priced in USD.

2. The Platform uses transparent multiplier pricing. The multiplier for each model is publicly listed on the "Models & Pricing" page.

3. Whatever model you request is the model that gets called. The Platform does not silently substitute models behind the scenes.

4. When your balance is insufficient, requests will return a 402 Payment Required error.

4. Recharge and Refunds

1. Recharged amounts are credited to your account balance immediately and processed through compliant payment channels such as Alipay.

2. Recharged balances are generally non-refundable. If a refund is necessary, please contact our support email with details.

3. The Platform reserves the right to handle long-dormant balances in accordance with applicable law.

5. Acceptable Use

1. You must not use the Platform for any activity that violates the laws and regulations of the People's Republic of China, including but not limited to generating illegal content, cyberattacks, or fraud.

2. You must comply with the terms of upstream providers (e.g. Anthropic). The Platform assumes you have lawful authorization to use such services.

3. You may not use the Platform's API for: automated mass registration, fraud / chargeback abuse, circumventing content moderation, or generating pornographic, violent, terrorist, or otherwise illegal content.

4. Upon discovery of any violation, the Platform may immediately suspend service, withhold any remaining balance, and reserves the right to pursue legal action.

6. Risk Control

The Platform applies rate limiting, anomaly alerts, and similar protections against abnormal requests (high-frequency abuse, stolen-card transactions, etc.). If your legitimate request is blocked by these systems, please contact support.

7. Limitation of Liability

1. The availability, response quality, and latency of upstream model services are not entirely within the Platform's control.

2. The Platform is not liable for service interruptions caused by force majeure (natural disasters, network failures, policy changes, etc.).

3. The Platform makes no warranty regarding model outputs. You are responsible for how you use such outputs.

8. Changes to These Terms

The Platform may revise these Terms in response to legal or service changes. Revised Terms will be posted on the website, and continued use constitutes acceptance of the updated Terms.

9. Contact

For questions, contact us via in-site feedback or email contact{'@'}transitai.cloud.
legal.privacyBody:
TransitAI Privacy Policy

Last updated: April 2026

The Platform takes user privacy seriously. This Policy explains how we collect, use, store, and protect your personal information.

1. Information We Collect

1. Account information: email address and a hashed (encrypted) password.

2. Usage logs: API request timestamps, model name, token usage, status codes, IP address, request latency, and similar technical data.

3. Payment information: recharge amounts, order IDs, payment method, and payment status. We do not store your bank card numbers or payment passwords.

2. About Conversation Content

1. The conversation content (messages) you submit through the API is forwarded in real time to the upstream model provider (e.g. Anthropic).

2. The Platform strictly prohibits selling or sharing user conversation content with third parties for model training purposes.

3. For troubleshooting purposes, some metadata (not full conversation content) is retained for up to 90 days.

3. How We Use Information

1. To provide and maintain the service (authentication, billing, rate limiting).

2. To improve service quality and perform statistical analysis.

3. To respond to lawful requests from law enforcement or judicial authorities when required.

4. Information Security

1. We use HTTPS for transport encryption, hashed password storage, one-way hashed API Keys, and similar security measures.

2. Servers are hosted with compliant cloud providers, and databases are backed up regularly.

3. While we apply reasonable measures to protect your information, please understand that no internet transmission can be 100% secure.

5. Cookies and Local Storage

The Platform uses localStorage to store login tokens (JWTs) for session continuity. Tokens are cleared on logout.

6. Minors

The Platform does not provide services to minors under the age of 14. If you become aware that a minor has registered without guardian consent, please contact us to have the account removed.

7. Your Rights

You have the right at any time to:

1. View your account information and billing history.

2. Change your password or delete your account.

3. Contact us via email to request export or deletion of your personal data.

8. Policy Changes

If we make material changes to this Policy, we will notify you via in-site notification or email. Continued use constitutes acceptance of the updated Policy.

9. Contact

Email: contact{'@'}transitai.cloud

errorLogs.title: Error Logs
errorLogs.sub: Last 50 failed/error requests · Click for details & solutions
errorLogs.tip400:
Bad request format.
· Check spelling of model parameter
· Verify messages array structure (role/content required)
· If using tools, ensure tool_calls is followed by matching tool responses
errorLogs.tip401:
Authentication failed.
· Verify API Key is valid
· Ensure Authorization: Bearer sk-xxx format
· Regenerate a key on the "API Keys" page
errorLogs.tip402:
Insufficient balance.
· Please top up on the "Recharge" page
· Or use a redeem code
errorLogs.tip403:
Forbidden.
· This API Key may have model access restrictions
· Or content filter/blacklist triggered
· Check "Allowed Models" in API Key settings
errorLogs.tip404:
Model not found or deprecated.
· Check model name spelling
· Verify available models on the "Models" page
errorLogs.tip413:
Request body too large.
· Max 10MB per request
· Split long text or compress images
errorLogs.tip429:
Rate limit exceeded.
· Free tier: 15 RPM / 10K TPM
· Pro: 60 RPM / 100K TPM
· Enterprise: 600 RPM / 1M TPM
· Upgrade tier or reduce request frequency
errorLogs.tip500:
Internal server error (rare).
· Usually temporary, retry later
· If persistent, contact support with request ID
errorLogs.tip502:
Upstream service temporarily unavailable.
· Upstream API provider outage
· TransitAI will auto-failover to other channels
· Retry in 30 seconds
errorLogs.tipUnknown:
Unknown error.
· See raw error message below
· If persistent, contact support with request ID
